deficits.missing: There is no single consolidated 'benchmark the transformer end-to-end' script matching CS336 A2 Part 1's deliverable: a script that times FORWARD and BACKWARD of the full model (not just the profiler's forward-only eval loop) across varying context lengths / model sizes, with warmup + torch.cuda.synchronize(), emitting a table of mean/std latency per config. The pieces exist but are scattered (torch.profiler eval loop in 4.1, do_bench in 4.4, memory_snapshot in 4.10) and none times the backward pass in a benchmarking harness.
deficits.fix: Add a ~40-line benchmark harness that builds a transformer, runs N warmup + M timed steps of forward AND loss.backward() via torch.cuda.Event(enable_timing=True), sweeps (context_len, d_model, n_layers), reports mean/std ms and peak memory (torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated after reset_peak_memory_stats), mirroring CS336 A2 benchmarking_script.py.

deficits.missing: The Kernel 4/5 implementation is FA2-style (forward parallelized over query blocks across the program grid, backward parallelized over KV blocks), but the chapter never explicitly ties its parallelization choice back to the specific FA-2 innovations (parallelize over the sequence-length dimension, minimize non-matmul FLOPs, warp-partition Q instead of K). A reader could build the kernel but might not articulate WHY it is 'FlashAttention-2' rather than 'FlashAttention-1'.
deficits.fix: Add 2-3 sentences (or a cross-link callout to 4.3) naming the three FA-2 changes and pointing out which lines of Kernel 4/5 realize the seq-parallel grid and the query-parallel forward / key-parallel backward asymmetry.

evidence: 4.4 Kernel 5 provides a COMPLETE runnable Triton FlashAttention backward: attn_bwd_preprocess computes D_i = rowsum(dO*O), attn_bwd_dkdv_dq recomputes P=exp(S-L) with correct dV=P^T dO, dP=dO V^T, dS=P*(dP-D_i)*sm_scale, dQ=dS K (atomic_add), dK=dS^T Q, plus torch.autograd.Function wiring.

4.4 gives exact base-consistency handling: forward folds log2(e) into qk_scale and uses exp2, then stores L=(m_i+log2(l))*ln2 as natural-log logsumexp so the backward uses a plain tl.exp -- and explicitly warns that mixing bases silently corrupts gradients.

4.4 correctness test diffs kernel gradients against an fp32 PyTorch-autograd reference for causal and non-causal, states expected tolerances (~2e-2 fp16, ~1e-3 fp32), and lists two named failure signatures (dropping D_i -> per-row constant dQ offset; dropping sm_scale fold -> gradients off by sqrt(d)).

4.4 handles edge cases: non-power-of-two HEAD_DIM (next_power_of_2 + mask offs_d<d), padding masks with other=-inf, l_i>0 guard against divide-by-zero on fully-masked tail, causal loop-bound skipping + triangular diagonal mask.

4.4 includes resource accounting (SRAM/register budgets for both matmul and attention tiles), autotuning configs, do_bench benchmarking, TRITON_INTERPRET debugging, and library mapping to the official 06-fused-attention.py tutorial and Dao-AILab/flash-attention.

3.5 provides a from-scratch TinyDDP with param/buffer broadcast, reverse-order gradient bucketing (25MB), register_post_accumulate_grad_hook, async all-reduce overlap, and finish_gradient_synchronization -- plus a verify_tiny_ddp.py that asserts bit-identical replicas (atol=0) and matches single-process training on the concatenated batch.

3.5 provides a from-scratch ZeRO-1 (TinyShardedOptimizer) with greedy-by-size ownership assignment, per-rank local AdamW over owned params, owner broadcast, a bit-exact (max_diff==0.0) verification against unsharded AdamW, and per-rank owned_state_bytes memory measurement.

3.5 gives the precise 16-bytes/param memory model table and a worked 7.5B-on-80GB example showing 120->31->17->1.9 GB progression across ZeRO-1/2/3, plus a complete runnable FSDP training script (auto-wrap, MixedPrecision reduce_dtype=fp32, FULL_SHARD, activation checkpointing, sharded-aware clip_grad_norm_, full-state-dict checkpoint).

4.1 profiling section: torch.profiler with schedule(wait/warmup/active), record_shapes, profile_memory, chrome trace export; nsys command with NVTX ranges; ncu --set full roofline; kernel_verdict() classifier; and the warmup+synchronize pitfall (cuda.Event / torch.utils.benchmark.Timer).
4.10 supplies memory benchmarking helpers: reset_peak_memory_stats, max_memory_allocated, memory_snapshot(label) -- covering the 'memory' half of the profiling deliverable.
summary: Reference-grade coverage of every A2-Systems deliverable. The Triton FlashAttention forward AND backward kernels (4.4 Kernels 4-5) are complete, runnable, causal-correct, handle non-power-of-two head dims and padding, get the exp2/natural-log base consistency exactly right, are wired into torch.autograd, and are validated against an fp32 reference with stated tolerances and named failure signatures. DDP-with-overlap and ZeRO-1 optimizer-state sharding each get a from-scratch, runnable, verified implementation in 3.5 (TinyDDP with bucketing/hooks/async all-reduce; TinyShardedOptimizer with greedy ownership and bit-exact verification), plus a full FSDP end-to-end script and precise 16-byte memory accounting. Profiling/benchmarking is covered in 4.1 (torch.profiler, nsys, ncu, roofline verdict, warmup/sync pitfalls) and 4.10 (peak-memory helpers). A competent reader could build all five deliverables from the book alone. The only gaps are minor: no single forward+backward end-to-end benchmarking-sweep harness, and the FA-2-vs-FA-1 partitioning rationale is left implicit. No correctness bugs found in the kernel or distributed code.
buildable_reasoning: Adversarially checked each deliverable. (1) FlashAttention forward+backward: both full kernels present with exact tensor shapes (B,H,N_CTX,HEAD_DIM), block-pointer arithmetic, online-softmax recurrence with alpha rescaling of BOTH l_i and acc, causal masking on both passes, atomic_add fp32 dQ, and driver + autograd.Function. Backward math (dV=P^T dO, dS=P*(dP-D_i)*sm_scale, dQ=dS K, dK=dS^T Q) is correct and D_i=rowsum(dO*O) is derived. (2) Correctness testing: eager reference + torch.allclose with dtype-appropriate tolerances and failure diagnostics. (3) DDP overlap: complete hook/bucket/async-allreduce implementation verified against single-process. (4) ZeRO sharding: complete, bit-exact-verified, with memory measurement. (5) Profiling: real profiler/nsys/ncu code and memory helpers. Nothing is hand-waved or left as pseudo-code.
